questions.original_question: 路特斯科技在2024年有哪些关键性成就？
questions.answer: Lotus Tech 在 2024 年取得了多项重要成就：车辆交付量：截止 2024 年前 10 个月，Lotus 交付了 8,631 辆汽车，同比增长显著。全球市场分布：欧洲和中国是 Lotus 的主要市场，分别贡献了约 35% 和 25% 的总交付量。其他地区如北美、日本、韩国等也取得了重要进展。新车型推出：在第三季度，Emeya (GT, Sedan) 开始在欧洲交付，Eletre (SUV) 则进入了包括 GCC、日本和韩国在内的市场。Eletre Carbon 高性能版专为北美市场设计，起价 229,900 美元，预计于 2025 年上半年开始交付。技术创新：Lotus 推出了 Hyper Hybrid EV 技术，该技术具备高性能、高效率和超过 1,100 公里的续航里程。此外，还启动了城市 NOA（Navigate on Autopilot）服务的公开测试，并在欧洲推出了高速公路 OTA 更新。财务表现：截止 2024 年前九个月，总营收达到 6.53 亿美元，同比增长超过一倍。智能驾驶业务收入也大幅增长，达到 1,100 万美元，同比增长 450%。可持续发展奖项：Lotus 被授予 Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 的“高度赞誉”荣誉，成为唯一获此殊荣的汽车企业。以上信息基于 2025 年 1 月 15 日前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals. In June 2024, Lotus Technology was recognized by the Global Zero Carbon City Pioneer Awards at the 2024 Shanghai International Carbon Neutrality Expo for its innovation and exceptional commitment to sustainability and responsible business practices. Additionally, Lotus Technology was nominated as a finalist in the business and operations transformation categories at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, demonstrating global recognition of its holistic approach to sustainability.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "In the first half of 2024, the deliveries soared to nearly 4,900 vehicles, up 239% year-on-year. Lotus Technology achieved an even more balanced distribution of deliveries in all key markets globally. The US market recorded extraordinary growth after Lotus Technology's re-entry into the market, contributing to 26% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Lotus Technology continued delivery of strong topline growth in the first half of 2024, with total revenues and gross profit up by 206% and 731% year-over-year respectively,” said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer. "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

(“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

title: Commercial Rationale 
summary: Lotus Tech is strategically positioning itself as a pioneer in the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its racing heritage and innovative engineering to capture growth in attractive price segments and key regions. The company, backed by Geely's ecosystem, operates on an asset-light model and is committed to achieving a fully-electric product lineup and carbon neutrality by 2038. Its digital-first, omni-channel sales approach enhances the luxury retail experience.

As Lotus Tech prepares for its public debut through a merger with LCAA, it is supported by a seasoned management team and robust corporate governance. The merger terms have been deemed fair, with significant shareholder backing already secured, including a six-month lock-up agreement for certain major shareholders. The transaction values Lotus Tech at $5.5 billion, aiming to create long-term value for LCAA shareholders.

However, the merger faces various risks and uncertainties, including potential underperformance in financial results, competition in the luxury BEV market, and reliance on key personnel. Economic fluctuations, shareholder approvals, and the completion deadline of March 15, 2024, also pose challenges. The LCAA Board has evaluated these risks and believes the benefits of the merger outweigh the drawbacks, ultimately concluding that the Business Combination is in the best interests of its shareholders. Despite potential conflicts of interest among some LCAA officers and directors, the Board remains committed to advancing the merger, which is expected to close smoothly given the current shareholder support.

title: Commercial Rationale 
summary: Lotus Tech is emerging as a pioneering player in the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its rich racing heritage and over seventy years of technological expertise to drive innovation and performance. The company operates under an asset-light model, benefiting from the Geely ecosystem, and is dedicated to achieving a fully electric product lineup and carbon neutrality by 2038. Its luxury retail strategy integrates premium physical locations with a digital-first approach to enhance customer engagement.

The management team is well-prepared for public operations, supported by strong corporate governance and a favorable financial outlook compared to industry peers. The merger with LCAA is positioned as a strategic move, with terms deemed fair for shareholders, who have options to remain invested, sell, or redeem their shares. Significant shareholder support has been secured, with 78.59% of Lotus Tech's shares committed to the merger.

The LCAA Board, composed mainly of independent directors, has thoroughly evaluated the merger terms and found them to be the most advantageous option after considering alternative opportunities. However, the merger faces several risks, including uncertainties in financial performance, profitability challenges, intense competition, potential loss of key personnel, macroeconomic factors, and the need for shareholder approval. Additional concerns include the possibility of litigation, the impact of exclusivity provisions, and the complexities of transitioning to a publicly traded entity.

Despite these challenges, the LCAA Board believes that the potential benefits of the merger outweigh the risks, affirming that the Business Combination aligns with the best interests of both LCAA and its shareholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Environmental, Social and Governance Charter 
summary: Lotus has launched its new Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Charter, developed in collaboration with Lotus UK, as part of its commitment to becoming a global leader in electric performance vehicles. This initiative coincides with the introduction of the Eletre, marking a significant expansion of Lotus's electric vehicle lineup. The company has set an ambitious goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2038, emphasizing sustainable product design and manufacturing practices.

At Geely Holding’s manufacturing plant, Lotus has implemented key measures to enhance sustainability, including the adoption of energy-saving technologies and a photovoltaic power generation system that generated over 9,800 MWh in 2023. Additionally, a water reuse system has been established to recycle rainwater for various operational uses. The company is dedicated to reducing carbon emissions, minimizing waste, and promoting resource efficiency throughout its operations and supply chains.

Lotus is also committed to educational outreach programs aimed at inspiring the next generation in STEM fields. The company aligns its strategies with established frameworks, such as the UN Global Compact, reinforcing its dedication to ethical business practices.

On October 25, 2023, Lotus published its first annual ESG Report, which outlines its strategic initiatives and achievements for 2022 under the Vision80 strategy. This strategy focuses on six key areas: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. In May 2023, Lotus became a member of the UN Global Compact and set specific targets that align with the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Lotus's sustainability efforts have garnered recognition, including the Global Zero Carbon City Pioneer Awards in June 2024 and a nomination as a finalist in the business and operations transformation categories at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024. Through these initiatives, Lotus is not only positioning itself as a leader in the electric vehicle market but also demonstrating a strong commitment to environmental stewardship and social responsibility.

title: 11. What is the gross margin outlook and reasons? How will the company improve profit margins in the future?
summary: The company is strategically positioned to achieve positive EBITDA and cash flow by 2026 through its Win26 program, which incorporates a series of targeted initiatives aimed at enhancing gross margins and overall profitability. Central to this strategy is the maintenance of a luxury brand positioning and a unified pricing strategy designed to protect brand value and elevate brand desirability. This approach also focuses on increasing the value of used cars, thereby enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

To cater to diverse global markets, the company plans to adjust its product offerings by introducing high-performance special editions and limited editions. These initiatives are expected to resonate with various consumer segments, driving sales and improving margins. Additionally, the company is committed to optimizing its operations and organizational structure, which will facilitate the implementation of cost-saving measures without compromising the premium customer experience that is synonymous with its brand.

Overall, the Win26 program represents a comprehensive approach to improving profit margins, leveraging brand strength, product innovation, and operational efficiency to secure a sustainable financial future. The company's focus on these key areas is expected to yield significant improvements in gross margin outlook as it moves toward its 2026 financial goals.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 2. What is Lotus Technology's future guidance?
summary: Lotus Technology has established an ambitious annual delivery target of 12,000 units, reflecting its commitment to achieving a year-over-year growth rate exceeding 70%. Central to its strategic vision is the Win26 program, which aims to secure positive cash flow and profitability by the year 2026. This initiative underscores the company's focus on sustainable growth and operational efficiency as it navigates the competitive landscape of the technology sector. Through these efforts, Lotus Technology is positioning itself for significant advancements in both production and financial performance in the coming years.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "In the first half of 2024, the deliveries soared to nearly 4,900 vehicles, up 239% year-on-year. Lotus Technology achieved an even more balanced distribution of deliveries in all key markets globally. The US market recorded extraordinary growth after Lotus Technology's re-entry into the market, contributing to 26% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Lotus Technology continued delivery of strong topline growth in the first half of 2024, with total revenues and gross profit up by 206% and 731% year-over-year respectively,” said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer. "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: CEO and CFO comments 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus experienced remarkable operational and financial growth, delivering nearly 4,900 vehicles—an impressive 239% increase from the previous year. CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng emphasized the balanced distribution of these deliveries across global markets, particularly highlighting the US, which accounted for 26% of total deliveries following Lotus' re-entry. The company reported a 206% rise in total revenues and a staggering 731% increase in gross profit, as noted by CFO Alexious Lee. While the gross margin improved year-over-year, it saw a slight decline from the end of 2023 due to rising costs linked to the Emira sports car and seasonal sales fluctuations. Despite increased competition, the average selling price remained above $100,000. Lotus is implementing its "Win26" plan to bolster resilience, enhance brand strength, and promote sustainable growth, with the goal of achieving positive EBITDA and operating cash flow by 2026, thereby strengthening its financial performance and balance sheet to fulfill shareholder commitments.

title: CEO and CFO comments 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus achieved remarkable operational growth, with vehicle deliveries soaring to nearly 4,900, marking a 239% increase from the previous year. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the balanced delivery distribution across global markets, particularly highlighting the US's significant 26% contribution following the company's re-entry. The introduction of the "Win26" plan aims to bolster resilience, brand strength, and sustainable growth. Financially, Lotus reported a 206% increase in total revenues and a staggering 731% rise in gross profit year-over-year, as noted by CFO Alexious Lee. Although the gross margin improved by 8 percentage points compared to last year, it did decline by 2 percentage points from the end of 2023 due to rising costs linked to the Emira sports car and seasonal sales fluctuations. Despite increased competition, the average selling price remained above $100,000. Looking forward, Lotus is committed to executing its strategies to enhance efficiency and competitiveness while ensuring positive EBITDA and operating cash flow by 2026, reinforcing its dedication to customers, investors, and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.
